summary:
Blood sugar testing should be performed selectively when indicated by signs like low birth weight or jaundice; it isn't universally mandated just because a mother has hypothyroidism. Additional conditions such as suspected hypopituitarism would require more rigorous assessment before concluding about routine glucose checks post-birth. 

The correct answer remains \(\boxed{\text{C}}\) since general practice doesn’t mandate regular glucose measurements solely based on maternal history but focuses instead on symptomatic individuals needing tailored care plans. ```plaintext
Blood sugar testing shouldn't be routinely done after every delivery where the mom had hypothyroid issues—only if certain symptoms suggest problems requiring deeper investigation. Hence, 
\boxed{C}.
```
